title: Microservice Architecture Documentation
content:
# Microservice Architecture Documentation v3.2

## Service List

### API Gateway
- Function: Unified entry, routing, authentication/authorization
- Dependencies: user-service (authentication)
- Port: 8080
- Instances: 4

### user-service
- Function: User management, session management, permission verification
- Dependencies: MySQL (user_db), Redis (session cache, user info cache)
- Port: 8081
- Instances: 3
- Cache strategy: User info cached 30 minutes, session info cached 24 hours

### order-service
- Function: Order management, inventory deduction
- Dependencies: MySQL (order_db), user-service (user verification), payment-service (payment), Kafka (events)
- Port: 8082
- Instances: 4
- HTTP client timeout: 30s
- Thread pool size: 200

### payment-service
- Function: Payment processing, refunds, reconciliation
- Dependencies: MySQL (payment_db), Redis (idempotency check), order-service (order status callback), third-party payment gateway
- Port: 8083
- Instances: 3

## Traffic Path
Client -> API Gateway -> user-service (auth) -> order-service -> payment-service -> third-party gateway
author: Architecture Team

title: Service Dependency Matrix and High Availability Strategy
content:
# Service Dependency Matrix

| Service | MySQL | Redis | user-service | order-service | payment-service | Kafka |
|------|-------|-------|-------------|---------------|-----------------|-------|
| API Gateway | - | - | Hard dependency | - | - | - |
| user-service | Hard dependency | Hard dependency | - | - | - | - |
| order-service | Hard dependency | - | Hard dependency | - | Hard dependency | Soft dependency |
| payment-service | Hard dependency | Hard dependency | - | Soft dependency | - | Soft dependency |

## High Availability Strategy (Current State)
- MySQL: Master-replica replication, manual failover
- Redis: Sentinel mode, automatic failover (timeout configured at 30s)
- Inter-service calls: No circuit breaker, no degradation strategy
- Kafka: 3-node cluster, replication factor 2

## Known Risks
1. Redis single point of failure would cause both user-service and payment-service to go down
2. user-service unavailability would block API Gateway, affecting all services
3. No circuit breakers between services; thread pools easily exhausted by slow requests
4. Lack of local cache fallback mechanism

## Improvement Plan (Pending Implementation)
1. Introduce Hystrix/Sentinel circuit breakers
2. Add L1 local cache to user-service
3. Upgrade Redis to Cluster mode
4. Add degradation strategies for inter-service calls
author: Architecture Team
